ddls:
CREATE TABLE "databases" (
  "db_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each database configuration */,
  "db_name" TEXT /* Name of the database */,
  "db_type" TEXT /* Type of the database (e.g., MySQL, PostgreSQL) */,
  "db_version" TEXT /* Version of the database software */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the database configuration was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the database configuration was last updated */,
  "environment" TEXT /* Environment where the database is deployed (e.g., production, staging, development) */,
  "environment_description" TEXT /* Description of the environment */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the database (e.g., active, inactive, maintenance) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("db_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "db_urls" (
  "url_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each URL */,
  "db_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the database configuration */,
  "url_index" INTEGER /* Index of the URL (e.g., 0 for the first URL, 1 for the second URL) */,
  "url_value" TEXT /* URL value for the database connection */,
  "url_type" TEXT /* Type of the URL (e.g., primary, secondary, backup) */,
  "is_encrypted" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if the URL is encrypted */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("url_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_db_urls_db_id FOREIGN KEY ("db_id") REFERENCES databases ("db_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "db_credentials" (
  "cred_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each credential set */,
  "db_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the database configuration */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username for the database connection */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password for the database connection */,
  "is_encrypted" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if the credentials are encrypted */,
  "last_changed" TEXT /* Timestamp when the credentials were last changed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("cred_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_db_credentials_db_id FOREIGN KEY ("db_id") REFERENCES databases ("db_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "db_versions" (
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each database version */,
  "db_type" TEXT /* Type of the database (e.g., MySQL, PostgreSQL) */,
  "version_number" TEXT /* Version number of the database software */,
  "release_date" TEXT /* Date the version was released */,
  "is_supported" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if the version is currently supported */,
  "end_of_support_date" TEXT /* Date when the support for the version ends */,
  "security_patch_level" TEXT /* Latest security patch level for the version */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("version_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "db_configurations" (
  "config_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each configuration setting */,
  "db_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the database configuration */,
  "param_name" TEXT /* Name of the configuration parameter */,
  "param_value" TEXT /* Value of the configuration parameter */,
  "param_type" TEXT /* Type of the configuration parameter (e.g., integer, string) */,
  "optional_value" TEXT /* Optional value for the configuration parameter */,
  "default_value" TEXT /* Default value for the configuration parameter */,
  "support_version" TEXT /* Minimum supported version for the configuration parameter */,
  "is_required" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if the configuration parameter is required */,
  "validation_rule" TEXT /* Rule for validating the configuration parameter */,
  "last_validated" TEXT /* Timestamp when the parameter was last validated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("config_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_db_configurations_db_id FOREIGN KEY ("db_id") REFERENCES databases ("db_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., DBA, Developer, Admin) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last login */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if the user account is active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the role */,
  "can_view" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if users with this role can view configurations */,
  "can_edit" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if users with this role can edit configurations */,
  "can_delete" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if users with this role can delete configurations */,
  "can_view_logs" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if users with this role can view audit logs */,
  "can_view_alerts" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if users with this role can view alerts */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "audit_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each audit log entry */,
  "db_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the database configuration */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user who made the change */,
  "action" TEXT /* Action performed (e.g., create, update, delete) */,
  "action_date" TEXT /* Timestamp when the action was performed */,
  "details" TEXT /* Details of the action (e.g., changes made) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the action was performed */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent used to perform the action */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_audit_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_audit_logs_db_id FOREIGN KEY ("db_id") REFERENCES databases ("db_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "configuration_history" (
  "history_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each historical configuration */,
  "db_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the database configuration */,
  "version" INTEGER /* Version number of the configuration */,
  "config_data" TEXT /* JSON object containing the configuration data */,
  "change_date" TEXT /* Timestamp when the configuration was changed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user who made the change */,
  "change_reason" TEXT /* Reason for the configuration change */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("history_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_configuration_history_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_configuration_history_db_id FOREIGN KEY ("db_id") REFERENCES databases ("db_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alerts" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert */,
  "db_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the database configuration */,
  "alert_type" TEXT /* Type of the alert (e.g., connection error, security issue) */,
  "alert_message" TEXT /* Message describing the alert */,
  "alert_date" TEXT /* Timestamp when the alert was triggered */,
  "resolved" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if the alert has been resolved */,
  "severity" TEXT /* Severity of the alert (e.g., low, medium, high) */,
  "resolved_by" TEXT /* User who resolved the alert */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_db_id FOREIGN KEY ("db_id") REFERENCES databases ("db_id")
)
